items.mistake: Skipping `pip install -e .` and then wondering why imports fail.
items.fix: Always install the package into your venv first; treat it as part of the setup, not optional.
items.why: The whole point of `src/` is that `import yourpkg` doesn't work without an install. That's the feature.
items.example:
```bash
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .
pytest -q   # now works
```

items.fix: Configure the build backend to look under `src/` (setuptools `packages.find`, hatch `targets.wheel.packages`).
items.why: Without it, the build backend won't find your package; the wheel will be empty or installation will fail.

items.mistake: Adding `src` to `sys.path` to 'make imports work without installing'.
items.fix: Don't — install the package instead. Manipulating `sys.path` defeats the layout.
items.why: It re-creates the flat-layout trap: ambient imports work locally but break in CI / for users.

items.mistake: Leaving the old package at the repo root after moving it into `src/`.
items.why: Both copies become reachable depending on `sys.path` order; you'll edit one and run the other. Painful.
items.example: After `git mv ragapp src/ragapp`, ensure no `ragapp/` remains at root: `ls` should not show it.

items.fix: Keep tests at the repo root in `tests/` (or another sibling of `src/`).
items.why: Tests are not part of what ships. Placing them in `src/` either makes them ship in the wheel or forces gymnastics to exclude them.
